constraints: Aarsi and Krypto were competing in a shooting game.
You are given a matrix AAA of size N√óMN \times MN√óM representing a shooting board. The cells of the matrix contain information about which player took a shot at which cell:
If A(i,j)=0A_{(i,j)} = 0A(i,j)‚Äč=0, no player took a shot at that cell.
If A(i,j)=1A_{(i,j)} = 1A(i,j)‚Äč=1, Aarsi took a shot at that cell.
If A(i,j)=2A_{(i,j)} = 2A(i,j)‚Äč=2, Krypto took a shot at that cell.
If A(i,j)=3A_{(i,j)} = 3A(i,j)‚Äč=3, both players took a shot at that cell.

Let the bullseye cell be (x1,y1)(x_1, y_1)(x1‚Äč,y1‚Äč), then, for a shot at cell (x2,y2)(x_2, y_2)(x2‚Äč,y2‚Äč), the score of the shot is calculated as max‚Ā°(‚ą£x1‚ąíx2‚ą£,‚ą£y1‚ąíy2‚ą£)\max(|x_1 - x_2|, |y_1 - y_2|)max(‚ą£x1‚Äč‚ąíx2‚Äč‚ą£,‚ą£y1‚Äč‚ąíy2‚Äč‚ą£). The score of a player is the sum of scores of all shots by that player.
Your task is to consider each cell in the matrix as the bullseye cell and find the value of ‚ą£|‚ą£Aarsi's score - Krypto's score‚ą£|‚ą£ accordingly.
Input Format
The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM ‚ÄĒ the number of rows and columns in the matrix, respectively.

The next NNN lines describe the matrix AAA. The ithi^{th}ith of these lines contain MMM space-separated integers ‚Äē the values A(i,1),A(i,2),‚Ä¶,A(i,M)A_{(i, 1)}, A_{(i, 2)}, \ldots, A_{(i, M)}A(i,1)‚Äč,A(i,2)‚Äč,‚Ä¶,A(i,M)‚Äč.
Output Format
For each test case, output a matrix BBB where B(i,j)B{(i, j)}B(i,j) denotes the value of ‚ą£|‚ą£Aarsi's score - Krypto's score‚ą£|‚ą£ considering (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) as the bullseye cell.
1‚Č§M‚Č§3‚čÖ1051 \leq M \leq 3 \cdot 10^51‚Č§M‚Č§3‚čÖ105
0‚Č§A(i,j)‚Č§30 \leq A_{(i, j)} \leq 30‚Č§A(i,j)‚Äč‚Č§3
The sum of N‚čÖMN\cdot MN‚čÖM over all test cases won't exceed 3‚čÖ1053 \cdot 10^53‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Aarsi took shot on cells (1,2),(1,3),(1,5)(1,2), (1,3), (1,5)(1,2),(1,3),(1,5) while Krypto took shot on cells (1,1),(1,3)(1,1), (1,3)(1,1),(1,3).
Considering (1,2)(1,2)(1,2) as the bullseye cell, Aarsi's score would be max‚Ā°(‚ą£1‚ąí1‚ą£,‚ą£2‚ąí2‚ą£)+max‚Ā°(‚ą£1‚ąí1‚ą£,‚ą£2‚ąí3‚ą£)+max‚Ā°(‚ą£1‚ąí1‚ą£,‚ą£2‚ąí5‚ą£)=4\max(|1-1|,|2-2|) + \max(|1-1|,|2-3|) + \max(|1-1|,|2-5|) = 4max(‚ą£1‚ąí1‚ą£,‚ą£2‚ąí2‚ą£)+max(‚ą£1‚ąí1‚ą£,‚ą£2‚ąí3‚ą£)+max(‚ą£1‚ąí1‚ą£,‚ą£2‚ąí5‚ą£)=4. Similarly, Krypto's score would be 222.
Thus, B(1,2)=‚ą£4‚ąí2‚ą£=2B_{(1, 2)} = |4-2| = 2B(1,2)‚Äč=‚ą£4‚ąí2‚ą£=2.
